checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture defines the task contract, selectors, snapshot builders, validators, batch orchestrator, independent review gates, reporting, and release boundaries; describes their one-way integration; and identifies Bash, PowerShell, JSON, Markdown, and jq while explicitly excluding service, database, frontend, and network runtimes.

design.md ## Architecture declares dependencies from orchestrators and review gates to selectors/validators and then to contracts/policies, and explicitly prevents selectors and validators from invoking agents or gates; this directed dependency flow contains no cycle.

design.md ## Data Plan provides Data Entities, Existing Data Affected, and Migration Strategy fields. It identifies all new versioned JSON contracts, snapshots, reports, and metrics; enumerates affected repository artifacts; and states that no database/application-data migration is required while defining atomic release and rollback handling.

design.md ## API / Contract Plan explicitly states that there are no network API, RPC, or event changes, lists the three new internal JSON contracts and stable diagnostics, requires unknown properties to fail closed, and states that no existing external endpoint is deprecated or breaking-changed. ## Backend Plan names the paired repository-local CLI surfaces.

design.md ## Security Boundaries enumerates task/policy-to-selector, repository-to-snapshot, manifest-to-agent, review-to-reviewer/evaluator, and rollback-to-working-tree boundaries with authorization or validation mechanisms, data classifications, and traversal, symlink, TOCTOU, injection, leakage, and tampering concerns. It also states that no credentials or PII are introduced; security-spec.md consistently refines the fail-closed allowlist and fresh-session controls.

design.md ## Test Strategy names unit/behavior, integration/parity, isolation-policy, templates/metrics, rollback, and acceptance/regression scopes with concrete test commands and CI evidence. It explicitly maps these commands to TEST-001 through TEST-006, covering the acceptance criteria without requiring a frontend user journey.

Every component referenced by design.md ## Architecture and ## Backend Plan is defined in ## Components, a dedicated subsection, or the requirements context: selectors, snapshot builders, validators, batch orchestration, reviewer/evaluator isolation, reports, release/rollback tooling, and host-managed agent launch responsibilities all have stated definitions.

design.md ## Architecture Decision Records lists docs/adr/0003-turn-first-agent-routing.md as the Accepted record for the feature's routing and context-isolation decisions, and no additional unrecorded architectural departure is identified. File-existence inspection was not possible because the ADR itself was outside the supplied allowed-input manifest; no contrary evidence exists in the permitted artifacts.
